title: Telegram reconnect — polling thread now starts even when network is initially blocked
current_state: TelegramTools.start_polling() did a one-shot _reachable() check at startup. If api.telegram.org was unreachable (VPN off, network blocked), it logged a warning and returned immediately without starting any polling thread. When the user turned the VPN back on, nothing retried — Pi stayed offline until a full restart.
target_state: start_polling always starts the polling thread; the unreachable check now only emits a softer warning. infinity_polling has its own internal retry loop and will reconnect automatically when the network returns.
fix_summary: tools/tools_telegram.py:start_polling — removed the early return on _reachable() == False
Reworded the warning to indicate retry is still happening
No new threads, no polling-interval changes — just removed the hard bail
